stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Materials scientist Nadia Okafor was developing a coating for satellite components used in cold and hot environments.
  • She measured the thermal conductivity, or ability to transfer heat, of the materials she planned to use.
  • Okafor found that aerogel composites transferred almost no heat and had a thermal conductivity of 0.015 W/(m·K).
  • She found that copper composites transferred large amounts of heat and had a thermal conductivity of 400 W/(m·K).
  • A material with high thermal conductivity moves heat through it more readily than one with low thermal conductivity.
stem: The student wants to contrast the thermal conductivity of aerogel composites with that of copper composites. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Materials scientist Nadia Okafor measured the thermal conductivity of the materials she planned to use in a coating for satellite components.
Aerogel composites and copper composites have different thermal conductivity values.
Aerogel composites have a thermal conductivity of 0.015 W/(m·K), whereas copper composites have a thermal conductivity of 400 W/(m·K).
A material with high thermal conductivity, such as copper, moves heat through it more readily than a low-conductivity material does.

rationale:
Choice C is the best answer. The sentence contrasts the thermal conductivity of aerogel composites with that of copper composites by stating each material’s value side by side.

Choice A is incorrect. The sentence describes Okafor’s general activity; it doesn’t contrast the two materials’ thermal conductivities. Choice B is incorrect. The sentence acknowledges a general difference but doesn’t specify the contrasting values. Choice D is incorrect. The sentence makes a general statement about thermal conductivity rather than contrasting the two specific materials’ values.
